title: bmd index default DB path doesn't match graph view's hardcoded read path
description: 'bmd index <dir>' with default flags writes to <dir>/.bmd/knowledge.db (internal/knowledge/commands.go's defaultDBPath, also used by cross-search). internal/tui/graph.go's NewGraphModel hardcodes filepath.Join(rootPath, "knowledge.db") - no .bmd/ prefix. Worse than just 'not found': knowledge.OpenDB() doesn't error on a missing file, SQLite auto-creates an empty one at that path, so the graph view silently renders 0 nodes with no error message. Confirmed pre-existing (git show 760be57^ has the identical hardcoded path), not introduced by Phase 32. Discovered/confirmed via manual smoke testing against the real binary.
acceptance_criteria: NewGraphModel reads from the same defaultDBPath convention (.bmd/knowledge.db) that bmd index's default and cross-search already use, so 'bmd index <dir>' (no --db override) works for graph view too.
notes:
## Steps to Reproduce
1. bmd index <dir>  (default flags, writes <dir>/.bmd/knowledge.db)
2. bmd --browse <dir>, press g to open graph view.
Result: graph view silently creates an empty <dir>/knowledge.db via SQLite auto-create and renders 0 nodes, no error shown.
close_reason: Exported knowledge.defaultDBPath as DefaultDBPath; NewGraphModel now uses it instead of a hardcoded rootPath/knowledge.db. Fixed the graph_test.go fixture to match. Full suite green.

title: Mouse clicks bypass activeChild routing, can silently corrupt Viewer file state
description: tea.MouseMsg is never routed through v.activeChild the way tea.KeyMsg/tea.WindowSizeMsg are (viewer.go ~901-903). Combined with v.links never being cleared in BackToDirectory()/BackToSearchResults(), an ordinary mouse click while browsing directory/search/graph can silently trigger followLink()->loadFile() against a stale link from the previously-open file. Viewer.FilePath/Doc/history get swapped with no visible change on screen since the child model still owns View(). Reachable through completely ordinary use (open a file, go back, click anywhere in the listing), not a contrived edge case. Full detail + fix sketch in .planning/phases/32-tui-mode-decoupling-directory-browser-cross-search/32-REVIEW.md (CR-01).
acceptance_criteria: tea.MouseMsg routes through v.activeChild when one is active, matching KeyMsg/WindowSizeMsg. v.links (and v.hasCursor) cleared in BackToDirectory()/BackToSearchResults() so stale link state can't outlive the file view it was built for.
notes:
## Steps to Reproduce
1. Launch bmd, open a file from the directory listing (Enter).
2. Press h to go back to the directory listing.
3. Left-click anywhere on the listing whose screen row happens to align with one of the previously-open file's link line offsets.
Result: Viewer.FilePath/Doc silently swap to the stale link's target with no visible change on screen.

title: Phase 31: Test & Reliability Debt
description: Fix 4 categories of pre-existing failing tests so go test ./... is green: (1) TestUpdateGraph_DownNavigates/UpNavigates/DownWraps/UpWraps in internal/tui — graph-view nav wrap bug; (2) TestResolveLink_ExternalLink/ExternalLinkHTTPS in internal/nav; (3) TestRenderDocument_Empty in internal/renderer; (4) TestOpenClawDescriptor_Valid/QueryCommand + TestDockerfile_Valid + TestDeploymentDocs_Valid in internal/knowledge, which reference a Dockerfile/openclaw.yaml/DEPLOYMENT.md that don't exist in the repo (investigate whether this is the same 'moved to graphmd, code never pruned' pattern before deleting).
design: See .planning/ROADMAP.md Phase 31 for full success criteria. Requirements: REL-01, REL-02, REL-03, REL-04.
acceptance_criteria: go test ./... is green (Steps to Reproduce: run go test ./... on main today, see the 4 failing categories listed in the description). Graph view Up/Down wraps correctly at boundaries; http(s) links resolve as external; empty markdown renders without error; internal/knowledge doc-artifact tests pass or are removed with their fixtures.
close_reason: Phase 31 verified passed — all 4 requirements (REL-01..04) fixed and confirmed on main

title: Unguarded narrow-width truncation panics (slice bounds out of range)
description: directory.go's renderDirectoryListing header and graph.go's truncateLabel both subtract a fixed margin before slicing with no floor guard (unlike splitPaneWidths()'s existing <80/<20 checks). directory.go panics when DirectoryModel.width < 3 (headerRunes[:m.width-3] goes negative). graph.go's truncateLabel panics when maxWidth < 1 for any non-empty title (called with width-8 and width-12 args). Full detail + fix sketch in .planning/phases/32-tui-mode-decoupling-directory-browser-cross-search/32-REVIEW.md (CR-02).
acceptance_criteria: Floor guards added before both slice operations; no panic for any terminal width >= 1.
notes:
## Steps to Reproduce
1. Launch bmd in a terminal narrower than 3 columns (directory.go) or resize a graph view to width < 8-12 columns depending on label length (graph.go truncateLabel).
Result: panic: slice bounds out of range.

title: Phase 33: Clipboard & Live Reload
description: Native OS clipboard fallback alongside OSC52 (view mode and edit mode) for terminals without OSC52 support. View mode detects external file changes on disk (fsnotify) and reloads, matching glow's behavior. Must not disrupt edit mode / unsaved changes.
design: Requirements: CLIP-01, RELOAD-01. Independent of phases 31/32/34 — small, low-risk, self-contained.
acceptance_criteria: Copy succeeds via native OS clipboard fallback when OSC52 unsupported; OSC52 path unregressed where supported. View mode auto-reloads on external file change; edit mode with unsaved changes is never disrupted by an external change.
close_reason: Both CLIP-01 (native clipboard fallback) and RELOAD-01 (fsnotify live-reload) implemented, tested, and committed. Full test suite green, gofmt clean.

title: Phase 32: TUI Mode Decoupling (Directory Browser & Cross-Search)
description: Directory-browser and cross-document-search modes become independent Bubble Tea child models communicating with the parent Viewer via messages, not shared boolean flags and direct method calls — mirroring glow's stashModel -> fetchedMarkdownMsg -> pagerModel composition pattern. OpenFileFromDirectory currently calls straight into Viewer.loadFile(); mode-transition flags (splitMode, graphMode, helpOpen) are shared across the directory<->graph<->help<->cross-search boundary.
design: HIGHEST-RISK phase this milestone — deferred twice already this session for exactly this reason. Must include an explicit design pass on the parent/child message boundary (what crosses as a message vs. what stays shared) BEFORE any code changes — see PROJECT.md Context and ROADMAP.md Phase 32 Note. Requirements: ARCH-01, ARCH-02, ARCH-03.
acceptance_criteria: Directory-browser and cross-search state live in their own model types with their own Update/render logic, not flat Viewer fields. File selection produces a tea.Cmd/tea.Msg the parent handles, not a direct loadFile() call. All existing directory-browser/cross-search functionality behaves identically (existing tests + manual smoke test pass).
close_reason: Phase 32 complete: all 4 plans executed, verified, UAT signed off. Follow-ups filed as bmd-51c/db6/2y3/0fe/js5.

title: switchModeMsg{modeDirectory} error path silently swallowed
description: When restoring/creating a DirectoryModel fails in Viewer.Update()'s switchModeMsg{modeDirectory} handler (viewer.go ~479-492), the error from NewDirectoryModel is dropped on the floor - no v.errorMsg set, activeChild left unchanged, user gets no feedback. The sibling modeGraph branch two cases above it correctly surfaces errors via v.errorMsg + clearErrorAfter. Detail in 32-REVIEW.md (WR-01).
acceptance_criteria: NewDirectoryModel error surfaces via v.errorMsg + clearErrorAfter, matching the modeGraph branch's pattern.
notes:
## Steps to Reproduce
1. Get into a state where DirectoryModel construction can fail (e.g. rootPath became unreadable/deleted mid-session) while switching modes back to modeDirectory.
Result: no error shown, activeChild left unchanged, screen looks unresponsive.

title: Phase 34: Rendering Evaluation Spike
description: Produce a concrete, evidence-based recommendation on bmd's markdown rendering approach: keep the custom internal/renderer, adopt glamour+chroma fully, or hybrid (chroma for code blocks only). Backed by a working prototype for code-block syntax highlighting. This is a spike/evaluation deliverable, NOT a committed rewrite of internal/renderer.
design: Requirements: RENDER-01. Scope guardrail: recommendation + prototype only, a full rendering-engine swap (even if recommended) belongs in a future milestone, not v1.1.
acceptance_criteria: Working prototype renders at least one real doc's code blocks with chroma highlighting, runnable independently of production render path. Written recommendation compares the 3 options against concrete criteria (visual quality, binary size, performance, maintenance) with a clear next step. internal/renderer production path is untouched by the spike. If glamour/chroma is recommended, follow-up implementation scope is written up.

title: Dead ASCII graph-diagram renderer: fix cycle handling or delete
description: RenderGraphASCII/renderGraphWithForceLayout/forceDirectedLayout/computeNodeLayout are fully written and unit-tested in graph.go but never called from the real View() path (renderFocusedSubgraph's list view is what's actually shown). Investigated wiring it up as the default; found a real bug first: computeNodeLayout's level-assignment relaxation assumes a DAG with zero cycle detection. On a cyclic graph (extremely common for interlinked markdown docs, e.g. a.md<->b.md), the relaxation never converges, node levels blow up (measured maxCol=1288 on a 200-node realistic graph vs expected ~0-1), and nodes land off-canvas and vanish silently with no error - 27.5% node loss measured in testing. The 40-node safety fallback to list view works correctly and is not itself buggy. Decision needed: (1) delete the dead code as a cleanup, or (2) fix the layout algorithm (proper cycle-aware leveling/compaction) and wire it up as a real diagram view, or (3) leave as untouched dead code (status quo).
design: Recommend (1) delete unless the diagram feature is specifically wanted enough to justify (2)'s real algorithmic work.
notes:
## Acceptance Criteria
Either: RenderGraphASCII/renderGraphWithForceLayout/forceDirectedLayout/computeNodeLayout and their tests are removed, and graph.go no longer carries unused rendering code; OR: computeNodeLayout gains cycle-aware leveling/compaction so cyclic graphs (e.g. a.md<->b.md) don't lose nodes off-canvas, and the diagram is wired up as View()'s real output (behind a toggle or as the new default).
